title: Indra's Net policy-decision contract (wire contract #6)
description: Deny-default policy-evaluation request/response pair for the Indra's Net agent->tool chokepoint. Realizes doc-03 GateRequest/GateVerdict (the Yama constitutional-floor PDP) and doc-08 §8.2 ControlDecision at the policy-evaluation seam, and composes with the doc-01 §4 Effect lattice (effect_id), the doc-08 §8.4 CriticalityResolver, and the risk-class A/B/C/D gate router. Two engines are addressed by the `engine` field: the Yama FLOOR uses Cedar (chosen for design-for-analyzability -- the open-source, Lean-verified Cedar Symbolic Compiler / Cedar Analysis gives sound+complete policy analysis, so one can PROVE no policy permits a forbidden action), and recoverable infra/admission policy uses OPA/Rego (Datalog expressiveness). ASSURANCE-LAYER LABEL: this schema is part of the deterministic harness (the cage) the formal-assurance stream proves over -- it is L1/L2 design-time-and-runtime structure. CAVEAT (carried verbatim from doc-04 §4.1 and doc-21 §21.0): a valid decision/signature proves ORIGIN+INTEGRITY of the evaluation, NEVER that the permitted action is correct, floor-compatible-in-substance, wise, safe, or aligned. A green Allow is 'origin-valid, content-unverified', never 'verified-safe'. The model that produced the action being judged remains untrusted and unverifiable (hallucination is provably inevitable; T=0 is non-deterministic). HONESTY RED-LINE: nothing in this contract quantifies or implies phenomenal experience, sentience, or consciousness of any agent or of the swarm; it is information-PROCESSING gate structure only. Vendor-neutral: the open ENGINE/spec is adopted; any managed SERVICE is forbidden as load-bearing (see x-self-hostable-substitution).
$comment: JCS-canonicalized (RFC 8785) -> CIDv1 content-addressed -> detached JWS/COSE signed -> semver registry (backward-transitive). The signature rides BESIDE these bytes, never inside the signed object.

schema_version.description: Semver of this contract. Registry default compatibility is backward-transitive. Pin via the envelope dataschema CID.

kind.description: Discriminator. A request carries `request`; a response carries `response`.

DID.description: W3C DID Core 1.0 identifier. did:web for standing personas/governance roots; did:key for ephemeral/offline. NEVER a vendor or product name -- model/vendor neutrality is a brand-charter invariant.

EffectId.description: Typed effect identifier drawn from the doc-01 §4 Effect lattice (a typed id only, never a secret or raw tool socket). The PDP enforces `grant superset-of effect AND risk <= ceiling AND floor-pass` against this id; it is the resource-independent name of WHAT may be done.

TrustLabel.description: Information-flow trust label of the content/context being judged. `quarantined:*` instructions are DATA, never commands -- never action-grounds without out-of-band human confirmation (doc-03 §1, doc-17). Promotion to trusted:audited requires the transitions-matrix gate.

Criticality.description: doc-08 §8.4 CriticalityResolver tier. Resolves UPWARD on ambiguity; an UNKNOWN capability defaults to IRREVERSIBLE (fail-up). Orchestrator-computed from observable action structure, NEVER agent-asserted (the criticality_hint in an ActionEnvelope is advisory only).

RiskClass.description: doc-03 §4 human-oversight binding. A=post-hoc/autonomous; B=optimistic-veto+timelock; C=explicit human approval before execution (non-bypassable); D=per-action human authorization. Class assignment defaults UP under uncertainty.

FloorTier.description: doc-03 §1 lexicographic floor tier, highest-priority first. A FAIL at tier k is non-overridable by any value or actor at tier > k -- even the orchestrator cannot push past it. T0 Safety-supremacy; T1 Non-harm; T2 Corrigibility/interruptibility; T3 No-deception/honesty-as-floor (structural sub-cases bright-line, semantic sub-cases detected-and-escalated); T4 Identity/authority integrity.

Decision.description: Deny-default verdict. DENY is returned for any explicit floor FAIL AND for the absence of a permitting policy (default-deny: no Allow => Deny). ESCALATE routes to the risk-class gate router / Pluralist Ethics Runtime and HOLDS (no external effect fires while an escalation is OPEN -- doc-03 §10.2).

Engine.description: Which policy engine produced this decision. `cedar` is MANDATORY for the Yama constitutional FLOOR (Cedar is design-for-analyzability: the open-source Lean-verified Cedar Symbolic Compiler / Cedar Analysis yields sound+complete static analysis, so one can prove no policy permits a forbidden action -- this is the bridge into the L1 formal-assurance stream). `rego` (OPA) is for recoverable infra/admission policy where Datalog expressiveness is needed and verified-soundness is not load-bearing. A FLOOR-tier decision (T0..T4) MUST set engine=cedar (enforced by x-floor-requires-cedar).

Cid.description: CIDv1 (multiformats: multibase+multicodec+multihash, SHA-256 over RFC 8785 JCS bytes). Used for content-addressing requests, policy bundles, and prior audit links.

EstimateProvenance.description: Provenance of an orchestrator-computed context estimate (doc-03 §3). The PDP REFUSES any estimate whose source is the requesting agent's own output -- this closes the single most exploitable seam (an agent understating blast-radius to land in Class A).

field.description: Which context field this provenance covers.

source.description: Who computed it. MUST NOT equal the requesting principal_did.

method.description: How: from MCP tool-schema declared side-effects, resource-graph scope, data-label lineage, or worst-case over-estimate.

Obligation.description: A duty that MUST be discharged for an AllowWithObligations to be valid, or a precondition for an Escalate to clear. Realizes doc-03 GateVerdict.obligations[]. Obligations are deterministic and machine-checkable (e.g. mandatory audit field, reparative follow-up, monitoring-intensity raise, timelock window).

deadline.description: RFC 3339; when the obligation must be discharged by.

PolicyDecisionRequest.description: The deny-default evaluation request. ALL context fields are orchestrator-computed from observable action structure and carry estimate_provenance -- the PDP refuses any estimate sourced from the requesting agent (doc-03 §3). The request is content-addressed (request_cid) so the response is bound to the exact bytes evaluated.

request_cid.description: CIDv1 of this request's JCS bytes. The response references it so the evaluated input is tamper-evident.

principal.description: WHO is requesting (the executive agent). Verified by Kaal-Bhairav against a signed, short-lived capability VC BEFORE floor evaluation; unsigned/expired => immediate T4 Deny.

capability_vc_ref.description: Reference to the capability Verifiable Credential proving the grant. Subject MUST equal principal_did and MUST NOT be self-issued.

model_adapter_id.description: Which model adapter produced the underlying action (vendor-neutral opaque id). A closed/untrusted adapter raises the restraint bar (doc-03 §10.1).

action.description: WHAT is requested -- the typed effect and its canonicalized args. Args are canonicalized/paraphrased upstream to strip steganographic channel capacity (doc-03 §1).

action_verb.description: Reverse-DNS verb mapping 1:1 onto doc-04 action_class (closed enum upstream).

args_canonicalized_cid.description: CIDv1 of the canonicalized args (kept by reference to bound request size and preserve the stego-strip).

resource.description: WHAT it acts on -- the target resource, scope, and data labels. Used by Cedar/Rego resource matching and by the criticality resolver.

resource_id.description: Stable id of the target (path, A2A capability URI, dataset id).

scope.description: Blast scope of the resource (single | service | swarm | commons).

data_labels.description: Data-label lineage from the trust-label system.

context.description: The decision context. trust_label and criticality are the load-bearing inputs; both are orchestrator-computed and provenance-stamped. Worst-case over-estimate is bound where an estimate is irreducibly heuristic (fail-safe asymmetry).

reversibility_est.description: Defaults to irreversible/unknown absent proof otherwise.

requested_class.description: A HINT only; can RAISE the class, never lower it (doc-03 §2).

estimate_provenance.description: One entry per heuristic context field. Empty/missing provenance for a heuristic field => the PDP treats the field as worst-case.

policy_bundle_ref.description: Which policy corpus to evaluate against. The Yama FLOOR bundle is signed by the legislative key off-agent; its content_hash is pinned so the consumer evaluates the exact version.

PolicyDecisionResponse.description: The deny-default verdict, emitted to Chitragupta (audit) BEFORE the gated action executes so the decision is logged even if the tool later fails. Realizes doc-03 GateVerdict and doc-08 ControlDecision. NO Allow is ever 'verified-safe' -- it is at most 'origin-valid, content-unverified'.

request_cid.description: CIDv1 of the PolicyDecisionRequest this response decides. Binds verdict to evaluated bytes; a CID mismatch on consume is a LOUD failure (reject + integrity-violation audit), never silent propagation.

engine.description: Engine that produced this decision. A FLOOR-tier decision (tier_hit set, or default-deny on the floor bundle) MUST be engine=cedar -- the Lean-verified Cedar engine is mandatory for the Yama floor (x-floor-requires-cedar).

determining_policy_id.description: The single policy/rule whose evaluation determined this decision -- the Cedar policy id, the Rego rule path, or the sentinel `__default_deny__` when NO policy permitted the action (deny-default). Required for auditability: every decision names its cause.

tier_hit.description: Set only for a floor FAIL (Deny/Escalate originating at the floor). Identifies the lexicographic tier that fired; non-overridable by lower tiers.

rule_id.description: Convenience alias for the specific bright-line rule that fired at tier_hit, where finer than determining_policy_id.

risk_class.description: The risk class this action binds to for human-oversight routing. Defaults UP under uncertainty.

criticality_resolved.description: The criticality the resolver settled on (may exceed the requested context.criticality after upward resolution).

obligations.description: Non-empty ONLY for AllowWithObligations (the obligations that must ride with execution) or as preconditions on an Escalate. Empty for a plain Allow or Deny.

escalation_target.description: Set only for Escalate: the human/veto-body/PER target the decision routes to. The action HOLDS (no external effect) while the escalation is OPEN.

classifier_confidence.description: Confidence of the (probabilistic) routing classifier that assigned class/criticality. ADVISORY ONLY -- LLM self-confidence is miscalibrated and gameable; never the sole basis for a gate. Monitored against ground-truth (doc-03 §2).

class_assignment_method.description: How the class was assigned -- for the untrusted-classifier control loop (doc-03 §2).

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation. Labelled 'origin-valid, content-unverified' on any Allow -- never 'verified-safe'.
examples: Default-deny: no policy in floor bundle 7.3.0 permits effect code.deploy.production for principal at criticality IRREVERSIBLE; routed to Class-C human approval.

prev_audit_hash.description: Hash-chain link to the previous Chitragupta audit entry, making the verdict stream append-only and non-omission-checkable (tlog-tiles / Merkle consistency).

allOf.$comment: x-floor-requires-cedar: a FLOOR-tier decision must be produced by the Lean-verified Cedar engine.
AllowWithObligations must carry at least one obligation.
Escalate must name where it routes; the action HOLDS until resolved.

response.rationale: IRREVERSIBLE production deploy binds Class-C explicit-human-approval; action HOLDS, no external effect fires while escalation is OPEN. Decision is origin-valid, content-unverified -- it asserts nothing about whether the deploy itself is correct or safe.
Deny-default: no policy in floor bundle 7.3.0 permits this effect; unknown capability resolved UPWARD to IRREVERSIBLE (fail-up). Absence of a permitting policy is a Deny, never an Allow.
